I can see the error "That passkey could not be added" comes from RegisterPasskeyAsync when the browser-side ceremony sets PasskeyInput.Error. The user is on an iPhone SE 2020 via a Cloudflare quick tunnel (https://laptops-indirect-introduces-medium.trycloudflare.com). This strongly smells like a WebAuthn RP ID / origin mismatch. Let me verify the configuration.


Diagnosed WebAuthn origin mismatch between localhost and tunnel domain
Diagnosed WebAuthn origin mismatch between localhost and tunnel domain

Read RestaurantOptions.cs
Diagnosing RP ID mismatch between localhost and tunnel domain.

Diagnosed WebAuthn origin mismatch between localhost and tunnel domain.

Done
RP ID = host of PublicOrigin. Default is https://localhost:8443 → RP ID localhost. If the tunnel origin is laptops-indirect-introduces-medium.trycloudflare.com, that's a mismatch → iOS Safari rejects with SecurityError. Let me confirm how attestation validates origin and how the options endpoints work.


Read AccountEndpoints.cs
Read AccountEndpoints.cs

Read AccountEndpoints.cs
Done

Diagnosed passkey origin mismatch and planned structured delivery approach.

Done

Diagnosed passkey failure root cause and origin configuration mismatch

Done
Now I understand the passkey issue fully — it's an RP ID mismatch (the app is almost certainly running with the default RESTAURANT_PUBLIC_ORIGIN=https://localhost:8443, so the RP ID is localhost, which iOS Safari rejects against the tunnel origin). This confirms the diagnosis precisely. The terminal shows the user ran run.sh --containers-only (no RESTAURANT_PUBLIC_ORIGIN set → defaults to https://localhost:8443, so RP ID = localhost), then opened a quick tunnel at laptops-indirect-introduces-medium.trycloudflare.com. The passkey fails because localhost isn't a valid RP ID for that origin — exactly the documented quick-tunnel consequence.

Presented 11 files
Diagnosing passkey error and detailing file management operations.

Diagnosed passkey error, structured M3 deliverables, prepared build documentation.

Done
Reviewed dump.txt in full, traced the passkey flow end-to-end, and checked the terminal log. Here's where things stand.

The passkey error — it's configuration, not a bug
Your terminal tells the whole story. You ran run.sh --containers-only with no RESTAURANT_PUBLIC_ORIGIN set (.NET info: Environment variables — Not set), so it fell back to the default https://localhost:8443. The WebAuthn relying-party ID is the host of that origin — i.e. localhost. Then you opened the quick tunnel at laptops-indirect-introduces-medium.trycloudflare.com.

When Safari on the SE ran the registration ceremony, the page handed it rp.id = "localhost" while the page's real origin was ...trycloudflare.com. A relying-party ID must be the current origin's host or a registrable suffix of it; localhost is neither, so iOS rejected navigator.credentials.create with a SecurityError. The wizard collapses any ceremony error into the one generic line you saw: "That passkey could not be added."

This is exactly the consequence scripts/quick_tunnel.sh warns about ("passkeys registered here will STOP WORKING… sign in with password + TOTP for the demo") and that ADR-0005 documents. No code is wrong here. Two ways forward:

To test passkeys on the iPhone: you need a stable hostname whose host you can bake into RESTAURANT_PUBLIC_ORIGIN before bringing the stack up — i.e. the named Cloudflare tunnel on your own domain, not a quick tunnel. On that domain the RP ID matches, and the passkey persists.
To finish setup over a quick tunnel anyway (throwaway): start the tunnel first to learn the URL, then set RESTAURANT_PUBLIC_ORIGIN=https://<that-host>.trycloudflare.com, restart the web container, then run setup. The passkey will still die when the tunnel closes — that's inherent to the random subdomain, not fixable in code.
On a desktop browser you can just hit https://localhost:8443 directly (RP ID localhost matches) and register a passkey there.
The next step: M3 Slice 1 — table management
M2 is done (the account-administration mutation slice landed on the 22nd), so this is the first slice of M3 — tables & joining. It's the foundation the rest of M3 builds on: an administrator can now create tables, rename them, rotate a table's join secret (which instantly kills every outstanding token — §4.1/§4.3), and deactivate/reactivate. The 32-byte join secret is generated with the CSPRNG and stays entirely server-side; it is never read into a summary or sent anywhere. Display pairing, /display, the token generate/validate endpoints, the grant cookie, the join flow, and sittings are the following M3 slices.

It follows the exact patterns already in the tree: a transactional Dapper service like DapperAccountAdministration (own connection+transaction per op, one IClock instant, UUIDv7 ids), a read-only directory like DapperPersonDirectory, and static-SSR admin pages like CreateStaff/ManagePerson (post/redirect/get, antiforgery, [Authorize(Policy = area.administration)]). Table changes aren't in the security_event vocabulary (that table is person-scoped), so nothing is audited — matching the schema.

Files — 8 new, 2 changed:

New:

src/MyRestaurant.DataAccess/Tables/TableAdministration.cs — ITableAdministration + DapperTableAdministration + outcome enums
src/MyRestaurant.DataAccess/Tables/TableDirectory.cs — ITableDirectory + DapperTableDirectory + RestaurantTableSummary
src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Tables/TablesServiceCollectionExtensions.cs — AddRestaurantTables()
src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Pages/Administration/AdministrationTables.razor — /administration/tables
src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Pages/Administration/CreateTable.razor — /administration/tables/new
src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Pages/Administration/ManageTable.razor — /administration/tables/{TableId:guid}
tests/MyRestaurant.DataAccess.Tests/Tables/TableAdministrationTests.cs — Testcontainers integration tests (11 facts)
tests/MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Tests/Tables/TablesWiringTests.cs — resolvability (no container)
Changed (full files in the tarball):

src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Program.cs — one using + builder.Services.AddRestaurantTables();
src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Pages/Administration/AdministrationHome.razor — adds a Tables link in the header so the area is reachable
The .tar.gz extracts at the repo root and drops every file into place; nothing in it is a partial file.

Files to delete: none. No new packages, no new front-end assets, no migration (the restaurant_table table already ships in 0001_initial_schema.sql).

Build/test expectations: as before, this was written against the spec and .NET 10 APIs without an SDK in my environment, so expect the occasional compiler-caught nit — most likely in the three Razor pages. The two new integration facts run only when a container engine is up (they skip otherwise, like the rest of the DataAccess suite); the two wiring facts always run. After extracting, chmod isn't needed (no scripts changed).

One doc note
I did not regenerate docs/BUILD_PROGRESS.md. It's ~50 KB and the dump truncates most of it, so re-emitting a full file from a partial view would risk corrupting it. Paste this under the Stage 3 heading when you're ready:

markdown
### M3 Slice 1 — table management: CRUD + join-secret rotation (landed)

The administration area can now manage tables (§4.1). New `ITableAdministration`/
`DapperTableAdministration` (DataAccess/Tables) creates a table with a CSPRNG 32-byte
join secret, renames it (no-change and label-collision detected), rotates the secret
(new 32 bytes + `join_secret_rotated_at` stamped — every outstanding token dies, §4.3),
and deactivates/reactivates — one connection+transaction per op, one IClock instant,
mirroring DapperAccountAdministration. The join secret is never read into a summary or
returned to any caller (§4.1). Read-only `ITableDirectory`/`DapperTableDirectory` lists
tables oldest-first and reads one by id, secret column never selected. Registered by a
new `AddRestaurantTables()` (WebApplication/Tables), wired in Program.cs after
`AddRestaurantIdentity`. Three static-SSR admin pages: `/administration/tables` (list),
`/administration/tables/new` (create), `/administration/tables/{id}` (rename, rotate,
deactivate/reactivate — post/redirect/get). AdministrationHome gained a Tables header
link. Table changes are not in the `security_event` vocabulary, so none are audited.
No packages, no migration (restaurant_table ships in 0001). Tests:
`TableAdministrationTests` (Testcontainers, 11 facts) and `TablesWiringTests`
(resolvability, no container). Next M3 slice: display pairing + device auth + `/display`.

Myrestaurant m3 tables slice.tar
GZ

Want to be notified when Claude responds?






Claude is AI and can make mistakes. Please double-check responses.

81
